details.context: Branch: "Circuit bug program" (open) — a tracked Dispatch conversation. Full source detail lives in the linked Dispatch doc (not in the tracker state).
details.instructions: PRIV-003 is a privacy-class bug surfaced by the audit-vocabulary / security sweep. The bug ID prefix follows the Circuit `<TAG>-NNN` finding-id convention. Look up the finding row in `docs/findings.md` (Circuit project) or the originating review doc to see the file:line, severity, and recommended fix.
details.recommendation: Don't ship a privacy fix without an explicit Misha decision — these usually touch user-data surfaces and warrant a Tier-2+ decision record per `~/.claude/rules/planning.md`.
details.blocking_input: Either schedule the fix (open a worktree, run the recommended remediation) or defer to a future privacy-cluster sweep. If unsure of the source row, ask Claude to grep `docs/findings.md` for `PRIV-003`.

details.context: Branch: "Circuit bug program" (open) — a tracked Dispatch conversation. Full source detail lives in the linked Dispatch doc (not in the tracker state).
details.instructions: A11Y-001 is an accessibility-class bug surfaced by the UX/audience audit pass. The Circuit `<TAG>-NNN` convention: look up the row in `docs/findings.md` or `docs/reviews/2026-05-13-ux-audit.md` for the file:line + recommended fix (typically a missing ARIA label, focus ring, or keyboard nav).
details.recommendation: Bundle with sibling a11y findings in a single sweep PR — A11Y-001 is rarely an isolated issue, and the audit usually surfaces a cluster.
details.blocking_input: Schedule the fix or defer. A11Y fixes are usually small (one or two attribute changes) and ship safely without an ADR.

details.context: Branch: "Circuit bug program" (open) — a tracked Dispatch conversation. Full source detail lives in the linked Dispatch doc (not in the tracker state).
details.instructions: JOURNEY-009 — Retell/voice setup signal — flagged P1 in `docs/reviews/circuit-onboarding-robustness-audit-2026-05-17.md`. Per Misha's prior call (backlog entry, 2026-05-17): kept OPEN, NOT auto-fixed, because 'fixing as a mandatory banner would recreate the JOURNEY-024 false-universal mistake; voice/AI is opt-in like ServiceTitan'. Decision needed: design a context-sensitive signal (only-when-relevant) OR leave dormant.
details.recommendation: Leave dormant. The current call (kept open, not auto-fixed) is correct — premature signaling re-creates the JOURNEY-024 'false-universal' bug. Revisit only when a contractor surfaces the friction.
details.blocking_input: Product-direction call from Misha: design the context-sensitive signal now, or leave dormant until voice/AI adoption surfaces real friction.

details.instructions: Demo prerequisite: confirm `RESEND_FROM_EMAIL` is set in Vercel prod env AND the corresponding DKIM record is published on the sending domain. Without DKIM, transactional emails (invitations, password resets) land in spam and the demo's white-glove invitation flow looks broken. Verify by sending a test invite to a personal email and checking inbox vs. spam + headers (DKIM=pass).
details.recommendation: Do this in the same sitting as the A2P portal paste — both are pre-demo prerequisites involving external consoles.

details.context: Branch: "Onboarding — contractor demo (tomorrow)" (open) — a tracked Dispatch conversation. Full source detail lives in the linked Dispatch doc (not in the tracker state).
details.instructions: Run `npx supabase db push` to apply migration 137 (`messages.external_id UNIQUE` per the WHK-001 idempotency fix). This was already pushed for the production org per the 2026-05-18 backlog entry — but if the demo org is on a different Supabase project, re-run there. Verify after: query `SELECT conname FROM pg_constraint WHERE conrelid = 'messages'::regclass AND contype='u';` — should show the UNIQUE constraint on external_id.
details.recommendation: Confirm first: is the demo org on the prod Supabase project? If yes, the migration already shipped (per backlog 2026-05-18 entry) — mark complete. If no, push to the other project.
details.blocking_input: Misha-only: needs Supabase CLI auth to the target project. If the demo org is on prod (single Supabase project per the backlog note), it's already done and this item can be marked complete.

details.context: Branch: "Pricing structure conversation" (open) — a tracked Dispatch conversation. Full source detail lives in the linked Dispatch doc (not in the tracker state).
details.instructions: INTERACTIVE CONVERSATION needed — Misha + Claude. No source doc exists yet because no decisions have been made. Topics to cover when ready: (1) tier definitions (Free / Pro / Enterprise — or different shape?); (2) per-tier feature gating (what's a tier vs an add-on?); (3) usage-based dimensions (per-org, per-conversation, per-message, per-AI-call?); (4) launch pricing vs. early-adopter pricing; (5) annual vs monthly discount shape; (6) public-page rollout (landing page, /pricing route, marketing assets).
details.recommendation: Block ~60–90 min for a focused conversation. Bring competitive landscape (ServiceTitan/Housecall Pro pricing pages) and a sense of the contractor's willingness-to-pay (from white-glove conversations).
details.blocking_input: Misha schedules an interactive Dispatch session to think through the structure. Until that happens, all downstream pricing work (landing page copy, billing integration, /pricing route) is blocked.
links: docs/backlog.md (the 'Pricing structure conversation' backlog entry tracks this)

details.context: Branch: "Phase 6 plan — preventive controls (DRAFT, needs Misha review)" (open). Sourced from docs/reviews/tcpa-decision-options-2026-05-17.md § "TCPA-001 — Quiet-hours computed against the wrong timezone on every SMS send".
details.instructions: Open `docs/reviews/tcpa-decision-options-2026-05-17.md` — 8 decision cards (TCPA-001 through TCPA-007 + TCPA-009). For each card, either pick A/B/C OR approve the drafted copy verbatim OR edit inline. The 'What you need from Misha to ship' line on each card is the single blocking input. Once all 8 are decided, the obvious-fix sweep ships them in one PR.

options.label: TCPA-001 — Quiet-hours TZ source
options.pros: A=Zip→IANA + NPA fallback (recommended; strongest legal posture, data already on contact); B=NPA only (smallest impl); C=stored contacts.timezone col + backfill (most auditable, biggest migration)
options.cons: A=zip→TZ table needs bundling ~400KB; B=number portability means wrong-TZ sends still possible; C=requires shared-prod migration

options.label: TCPA-001 fail-closed policy
options.pros: BLOCK marketing when TZ unresolved (recommended); transactional still sends

options.label: TCPA-002 — Scheduled sends use recipient TZ, not org TZ (decide together with TCPA-001)
TCPA-003..006 (+ P1s TCPA-007, TCPA-009)
options.pros: See cards in the doc — each has 3 options + recommendation + drafted copy

details.recommendation: Read the doc once end-to-end, decide all 8 in one sitting, hand back. The sweep PR is already specced — only the policy choices block it.
details.blocking_input: 8 A/B/C decisions from Misha + sign-off on drafted copy for TCPA-007 (HELP/STOP) and TCPA-009. Legal-claim honesty: $500–$1,500/text under 47 U.S.C. § 227(b)(3); quiet-hours-applied-to-SMS is plaintiff practice [verify with counsel] for exact theory.

details.context: Branch: "Phase 6 plan — preventive controls (DRAFT, needs Misha review)" (open). Sourced from docs/plans/phase-6-preventive-controls.md § "Files to Modify/Create (Phase-6 build — NOT this session)".
details.instructions: Open `docs/plans/phase-6-preventive-controls.md` (Status: DRAFT). Read the Goal + Scope + Why-now sections. The plan unifies three preventive layers: (A) per-org cost/rate ceiling — fail-closed BEFORE spend, not after; (B) error-spine alerter — push critical `system_errors` to a human; (C) `webhook_events` dedup ledger — structural at-least-once replay protection. Hard dependency: PR #249 (`claude/admiring-mcnulty-f1c358`) must merge to master first — Phase-6 builds on top of it. Once Misha ratifies, flip Status DRAFT → ACTIVE and the build can begin.
details.recommendation: Don't ratify until PR #249 lands (the plan has a hard build-on dependency). Ratifying earlier risks the foundation moving under the plan.
details.blocking_input: Misha-only: read the plan, decide whether to ratify as-written, request changes, or split into smaller phases. The 'open questions' section at the bottom of the plan lists specific unresolved items needing input.

details.context: Branch: "Manual portal actions — paste-ready (waiting on Misha)" (open) — a tracked Dispatch conversation. Full source detail lives in the linked Dispatch doc (not in the tracker state).
details.instructions: Open the Twilio Console (SSO) and execute Steps 1–6 of `docs/twilio-a2p-manual-actions-misha-2026-05-17.md`: (1) Confirm the A2P Brand is APPROVED, note the TCR Brand ID + Trust Score; (2) Submit/refresh the Campaign (the §1 CTA hard-blocker is already cleared on prod — `/sms-terms`, `/api/request-service`, `/privacy`, `/terms` all live & green); (3) Associate the messaging service to the campaign; (4) Confirm the long code is registered under that campaign; (5) Verify by sending one test SMS via the Twilio console; (6) Update the org's `twilio_config.account_sid` if it changed during registration. All paste-ready copy + paste targets are in the doc.
details.recommendation: Execute in order — each step depends on the previous one. Total elapsed: ~30–45 min if Brand is already APPROVED, longer if Brand is PENDING (then you wait for Twilio, not for execution).
details.blocking_input: Misha-only: Twilio Console SSO is required for every step. None of this can be done by Claude.

details.context: Branch: "TWLO-006 launch-blocker — wire demo org twilio_config (waiting on Misha)" (open) — a tracked Dispatch conversation. Full source detail lives in the linked Dispatch doc (not in the tracker state).
details.instructions: On the demo / onboarded org set `twilio_config.phone_number="+19493905976"` AND align `twilio_config.account_sid` + per-org Vault `twilio_auth_token` to the active Twilio account (last-4 `…66c9`). The inbound signature check uses global `process.env.TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN`, so the owning org's account MUST match that env token (TWLO-004). Until this lands, `handleInboundSms` returns `org_not_found` → silent empty-TwiML 200, and every real inbound SMS (replies/STOP/HELP/AI) is dropped regardless of TWLO-001.
details.recommendation: Do this BEFORE the demo. Without it the demo's inbound SMS path is silently broken — no error, just empty 200s and no AI replies.
details.blocking_input: Launch-blocker for tomorrow's demo. No code change — Misha needs to update the demo org's `twilio_config` row in Supabase prod (`organizations.twilio_config->>phone_number = +19493905976`).
links: (see branch: TWLO-006 launch-blocker — wire demo org twilio_config (waiting on Misha))

events.text: Sign up for Sentry — paste back DSN, auth-token, org & project slugs

details.description: Sentry is the proposed error-tracking backend for the customer-facing app. Orchestrator cannot provision the account; needs Misha to register and return the credentials so the integration can be wired.
details.instructions: 1) Create a Sentry account (or use an existing one) at sentry.io. 2) Create a new project for the customer-facing app (TypeScript/Next.js). 3) Copy: (a) the DSN, (b) an auth-token with project:write + project:read scopes (Settings → Auth Tokens → Create New), (c) the organization slug, (d) the project slug. 4) Paste all four back in a Dispatch reply.
details.recommendation: Use the Team plan if you anticipate >5k events/month; Developer plan is fine for the launch sprint.
details.blocking_input: Sentry DSN + auth-token + org slug + project slug

events.text: Sign up for Axiom — paste back ingest token

details.description: Axiom is the proposed structured-log backend (paired with Sentry for errors). Orchestrator needs an ingest token to wire the log shipper.
details.instructions: 1) Create an Axiom account at axiom.co. 2) Create a dataset (suggest: customer-app-prod). 3) Generate an API token with ingest permission on that dataset (Settings → API Tokens). 4) Paste the token + dataset name back in a Dispatch reply.
details.recommendation: Free tier covers up to 500GB/month — comfortable for launch + first 90 days.
details.blocking_input: Axiom ingest token + dataset name

events.text: Choose between (a) bisect, (b) hosted-routes-only, (c) full Vercel migration

details.description: The customer-app's hosting story has three credible paths forward. Each carries a different blast-radius + reversibility tradeoff. Decision is needed before the Vercel-migration comparison work can start.

options.label: (a) Bisect the current deploy issue first
options.pros: Cheapest; isolates the actual root cause; doesn't commit to a platform shift
options.cons: Doesn't resolve the long-term hosting question; may surface a fix that's specific to today's symptom rather than tomorrow's class

options.label: (b) Migrate hosted routes only
options.pros: Smaller blast radius than full migration; lets us evaluate Vercel on a subset; reversible per-route
options.cons: Splits the app across two hosts; ops complexity (two dashboards, two deploys); env-var duplication risk

options.label: (c) Full Vercel migration
options.pros: Single hosting story; first-class Next.js features (ISR, edge, image optimization); simplest mental model
options.cons: Largest blast radius; harder to reverse if Vercel's pricing/limits surprise us; lock-in to Vercel-specific primitives

details.recommendation: Orchestrator's tentative lean is (a) bisect first — get the immediate symptom resolved, then make the platform choice with that data in hand. But this needs Misha's call.
details.blocking_input: Misha picks (a), (b), or (c) — and if (b)/(c), authorizes the scoping work.

events.text: Want me to scope the Vercel-migration comparison doc?

details.description: Before committing to (b) or (c), the orchestrator can produce a comparison doc (current host vs. Vercel: feature parity, cost model, ops surface, observability hooks, rollback story). The work is ~2-4 hours.
details.instructions: Reply: yes / no / not-yet. If yes, the orchestrator will draft the doc and surface it as a follow-up item with the comparison filled in.
details.recommendation: Worth doing IF Misha leans (b) or (c). Skip if (a) bisect-first.
details.blocking_input: Misha's go/no-go on scoping the doc

events.text: Subscribe to vercel-status.com so we get pinged on incidents

details.description: Independent of the migration decision, subscribing to Vercel status alerts is cheap insurance — if Vercel's hosted-routes platform is in our serving chain, we want a heads-up on incidents.
details.instructions: Go to vercel-status.com → Subscribe to Updates → enter Misha's email or a shared inbox. Choose channels (Email / RSS / Slack webhook).
details.recommendation: Email subscription to the on-call address; takes <60 seconds.
details.blocking_input: Misha completes subscription (no follow-up needed — small task, fire-and-forget).

events.text: RESOLVED (2026-05-28): root cause was NOT a Vercel/hosting problem � it was a Next.js dynamic-segment slug conflict ([id] vs [orgId]), fixed in code by PR #309. Migration is superseded + closed (ADR docs/decisions/063-vercel-stays-no-migration.md). Branch left open ONLY for the cause-independent action i-vercel-status-sub (subscribe to vercel-status.com).
S1 Auth & Access (P0): login/forgot-password repair, Google sign-in + post-invite refresh bug, "you already exist" re-invite block, bulk invite 1-of-4 � C-07/C-08/C-47/C-31
S2 Org Form Hardening (P0): Industry+Timezone dropdowns on /admin/orgs, hide slug field, jsdom+RTL component-test infra � WALKTHROUGH-001 = C-01/C-02
S3 Search/Save/Persistence (P0): fix contact search, contact-create persistence, Personal Data save no-op, member-role update, standardize save UX � C-12/C-41/C-21/C-32/C-35
S4 Contact Import Hardening (P0): address mapping, full-name split, per-row error reporting, phone + prefix � C-15/C-10/C-11/C-16 (overlaps S3 contact pages)
S5 CallRail Integration (P0): API scoping+wiring, RingCentral compat, demonstrable end-to-end for One Season � C-04/C-06 (biggest blocker)
Tier 2 (queued): Schedules & Availability, dynamic import auto-mapping (C-09), Comm-Prefs overhaul (C-25/26/27/28/29), AI-suggested message (C-17), test-orgs purge (C-03), misc UX bundle
Tier 3 (Architecture, Jaime): PRD draft from build doctrine + code audit, 22-contradiction guardrails ship, conversation engine principles
COORD � Cody: provide real contact export file (blocks S4 import verification)
COORD � Jaime: ship 22-contradiction guardrails + PRD draft (Tier 3) + CallRail/RingCentral docs C-05 (blocks S5)
COORD � Misha: review CallRail scope before merge (S5); decide which test orgs to purge (C-03)

DECIDED 2026-05-29: CallRail webhook auth = per-org unguessable URL token (matches Retell handler pattern; no HMAC because CallRail does not expose a usable signature mechanism). Documented in PR #371 scoping doc.
DECIDED 2026-05-29: RingCentral integration NOT needed � CallRail abstracts RingCentral. S5 scope reduced (C-06 dropped from S5 sub-items).

DECIDED 2026-05-29: Each email maps to ONE org + ONE user. C-47 implements as inline invite-UI warning � (a) "this email already has an account", (b) same-org vs different-org context with org name visible to inviter, (c) "move account here" affordance. No new screens.

Tier 2: Contact deletion/archive (verified gap � no DELETE handler at src/app/api/contacts/[id]/route.ts, bulk-action route supports only move_stage + send_message, no soft-delete fields, no UI delete button). Recommend soft-archive default. Owner-only with admin override per org policy. UI surfaces: contact detail (single), list (bulk), confirmation modal.

Tier 2: S8 � In-app support chatbot. Floating Help widget on every Circuit page. Phase 1 = Q&A over Support Pages MDX corpus. Phase 2 = tool-call extension (show-me-where-X-is, explain-this-error, summarize-org-state, whats-changed-recently). Uses Circuit LLM client. Per-user conversation history. Plugin interface for new tools. Was BLOCKED on S7 Phase 2 ? SHIPPED in PR #378 ? UNBLOCKED 2026-05-29.

DECIDED 2026-05-29: Contact deletion = soft-archive ONLY on contractor surface (archived_at, hidden from list/search, reversible by admin, audit trail preserved). Hard-delete reserved for verified CCPA requests via dedicated admin-only workflow (structured request log, 24-month retention, third-party vendor notification trigger). Hard-delete NOT exposed in regular contact UI.

HF-1 (Hotfix): disable test-org generation in prod. Circuit creating circuit-test* orgs daily. Session local_65df9ac4-433d-4465-9a1f-a24dfb42cdc0. 6 steps: identify source ? disable via PR ? count accumulated test orgs ? soft-archive them ? add guard against silent re-enable ? 24h regression watch.
OPEN (Misha decision): is the CCPA-request workflow in Tier 2 (pre-launch) or Tier 3? Circuit serves California contractors + California consumers; Misha leans Tier 2.

items.text: PRIV-003 (older open)

details.context: Branch: "Circuit bug program" (open) — a tracked Dispatch conversation. Full source detail lives in the linked Dispatch doc (not in the tracker state).
details.instructions: PRIV-003 is a privacy-class bug surfaced by the audit-vocabulary / security sweep. The bug ID prefix follows the Circuit `<TAG>-NNN` finding-id convention. Look up the finding row in `docs/findings.md` (Circuit project) or the originating review doc to see the file:line, severity, and recommended fix.
details.recommendation: Don't ship a privacy fix without an explicit Misha decision — these usually touch user-data surfaces and warrant a Tier-2+ decision record per `~/.claude/rules/planning.md`.
details.blocking_input: Either schedule the fix (open a worktree, run the recommended remediation) or defer to a future privacy-cluster sweep. If unsure of the source row, ask Claude to grep `docs/findings.md` for `PRIV-003`.

items.text: A11Y-001 (older open)

details.context: Branch: "Circuit bug program" (open) — a tracked Dispatch conversation. Full source detail lives in the linked Dispatch doc (not in the tracker state).
details.instructions: A11Y-001 is an accessibility-class bug surfaced by the UX/audience audit pass. The Circuit `<TAG>-NNN` convention: look up the row in `docs/findings.md` or `docs/reviews/2026-05-13-ux-audit.md` for the file:line + recommended fix (typically a missing ARIA label, focus ring, or keyboard nav).
details.recommendation: Bundle with sibling a11y findings in a single sweep PR — A11Y-001 is rarely an isolated issue, and the audit usually surfaces a cluster.
details.blocking_input: Schedule the fix or defer. A11Y fixes are usually small (one or two attribute changes) and ship safely without an ADR.

items.text: JOURNEY-009 (older open)

details.context: Branch: "Circuit bug program" (open) — a tracked Dispatch conversation. Full source detail lives in the linked Dispatch doc (not in the tracker state).

Per Misha's prior call (backlog entry, 2026-05-17): kept OPEN, NOT auto-fixed, because 'fixing as a mandatory banner would recreate the JOURNEY-024 false-universal mistake; voice/AI is opt-in like ServiceTitan'. Decision needed: design a context-sensitive signal (only-when-relevant) OR leave dormant.

items.text: TCPA: 8 decision cards — docs/reviews/tcpa-decision-options-2026-05-17.md

details.context: Branch: "Phase 6 plan — preventive controls (DRAFT, needs Misha review)" (open). Sourced from docs/reviews/tcpa-decision-options-2026-05-17.md § "TCPA-001 — Quiet-hours computed against the wrong timezone on every SMS send".
details.instructions: Open `docs/reviews/tcpa-decision-options-2026-05-17.md` — 8 decision cards (TCPA-001 through TCPA-007 + TCPA-009). For each card, either pick A/B/C OR approve the drafted copy verbatim OR edit inline. The 'What you need from Misha to ship' line on each card is the single blocking input. Once all 8 are decided, the obvious-fix sweep ships them in one PR.

options.label: TCPA-001 — Quiet-hours TZ source
options.pros: A=Zip→IANA + NPA fallback (recommended; strongest legal posture, data already on contact); B=NPA only (smallest impl); C=stored contacts.timezone col + backfill (most auditable, biggest migration)
options.cons: A=zip→TZ table needs bundling ~400KB; B=number portability means wrong-TZ sends still possible; C=requires shared-prod migration

options.label: TCPA-001 fail-closed policy
options.pros: BLOCK marketing when TZ unresolved (recommended); transactional still sends

options.label: TCPA-002 — Scheduled sends use recipient TZ, not org TZ (decide together with TCPA-001)
TCPA-003..006 (+ P1s TCPA-007, TCPA-009)
options.pros: See cards in the doc — each has 3 options + recommendation + drafted copy

details.recommendation: Read the doc once end-to-end, decide all 8 in one sitting, hand back. The sweep PR is already specced — only the policy choices block it.
details.blocking_input: 8 A/B/C decisions from Misha + sign-off on drafted copy for TCPA-007 (HELP/STOP) and TCPA-009. Legal-claim honesty: $500–$1,500/text under 47 U.S.C. § 227(b)(3); quiet-hours-applied-to-SMS is plaintiff practice [verify with counsel] for exact theory.

items.text: Phase 6 plan ratification — docs/plans/phase-6-preventive-controls.md

details.context: Branch: "Phase 6 plan — preventive controls (DRAFT, needs Misha review)" (open). Sourced from docs/plans/phase-6-preventive-controls.md § "Files to Modify/Create (Phase-6 build — NOT this session)".
details.instructions: Open `docs/plans/phase-6-preventive-controls.md` (Status: DRAFT). Read the Goal + Scope + Why-now sections. The plan unifies three preventive layers: (A) per-org cost/rate ceiling — fail-closed BEFORE spend, not after; (B) error-spine alerter — push critical `system_errors` to a human; (C) `webhook_events` dedup ledger — structural at-least-once replay protection. Hard dependency: PR #249 (`claude/admiring-mcnulty-f1c358`) must merge to master first — Phase-6 builds on top of it. Once Misha ratifies, flip Status DRAFT → ACTIVE and the build can begin.
details.recommendation: Don't ratify until PR #249 lands (the plan has a hard build-on dependency). Ratifying earlier risks the foundation moving under the plan.
details.blocking_input: Misha-only: read the plan, decide whether to ratify as-written, request changes, or split into smaller phases. The 'open questions' section at the bottom of the plan lists specific unresolved items needing input.

nodes.title: TWLO-006 launch-blocker — wire demo org twilio_config (waiting on Misha)

items.text: Wire demo org's twilio_config (launch-blocker)

details.context: Branch: "TWLO-006 launch-blocker — wire demo org twilio_config (waiting on Misha)" (open) — a tracked Dispatch conversation. Full source detail lives in the linked Dispatch doc (not in the tracker state).
details.instructions: On the demo / onboarded org set `twilio_config.phone_number="+19493905976"` AND align `twilio_config.account_sid` + per-org Vault `twilio_auth_token` to the active Twilio account (last-4 `…66c9`). The inbound signature check uses global `process.env.TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN`, so the owning org's account MUST match that env token (TWLO-004). Until this lands, `handleInboundSms` returns `org_not_found` → silent empty-TwiML 200, and every real inbound SMS (replies/STOP/HELP/AI) is dropped regardless of TWLO-001.
details.recommendation: Do this BEFORE the demo. Without it the demo's inbound SMS path is silently broken — no error, just empty 200s and no AI replies.
details.blocking_input: Launch-blocker for tomorrow's demo. No code change — Misha needs to update the demo org's `twilio_config` row in Supabase prod (`organizations.twilio_config->>phone_number = +19493905976`).
links: (see branch: TWLO-006 launch-blocker — wire demo org twilio_config (waiting on Misha))

items.text: Sign up for Sentry — paste back DSN, auth-token, org & project slugs

details.description: Sentry is the proposed error-tracking backend for the customer-facing app. Orchestrator cannot provision the account; needs Misha to register and return the credentials so the integration can be wired.
details.instructions: 1) Create a Sentry account (or use an existing one) at sentry.io. 2) Create a new project for the customer-facing app (TypeScript/Next.js). 3) Copy: (a) the DSN, (b) an auth-token with project:write + project:read scopes (Settings → Auth Tokens → Create New), (c) the organization slug, (d) the project slug. 4) Paste all four back in a Dispatch reply.
details.recommendation: Use the Team plan if you anticipate >5k events/month; Developer plan is fine for the launch sprint.
details.blocking_input: Sentry DSN + auth-token + org slug + project slug

items.text: Sign up for Axiom — paste back ingest token

details.description: Axiom is the proposed structured-log backend (paired with Sentry for errors). Orchestrator needs an ingest token to wire the log shipper.
details.instructions: 1) Create an Axiom account at axiom.co. 2) Create a dataset (suggest: customer-app-prod). 3) Generate an API token with ingest permission on that dataset (Settings → API Tokens). 4) Paste the token + dataset name back in a Dispatch reply.
details.recommendation: Free tier covers up to 500GB/month — comfortable for launch + first 90 days.
details.blocking_input: Axiom ingest token + dataset name

items.text: Choose between (a) bisect, (b) hosted-routes-only, (c) full Vercel migration

details.description: The customer-app's hosting story has three credible paths forward. Each carries a different blast-radius + reversibility tradeoff. Decision is needed before the Vercel-migration comparison work can start.

options.label: (a) Bisect the current deploy issue first
options.pros: Cheapest; isolates the actual root cause; doesn't commit to a platform shift
options.cons: Doesn't resolve the long-term hosting question; may surface a fix that's specific to today's symptom rather than tomorrow's class

options.label: (b) Migrate hosted routes only
options.pros: Smaller blast radius than full migration; lets us evaluate Vercel on a subset; reversible per-route
options.cons: Splits the app across two hosts; ops complexity (two dashboards, two deploys); env-var duplication risk

options.label: (c) Full Vercel migration
options.pros: Single hosting story; first-class Next.js features (ISR, edge, image optimization); simplest mental model
options.cons: Largest blast radius; harder to reverse if Vercel's pricing/limits surprise us; lock-in to Vercel-specific primitives

details.recommendation: Orchestrator's tentative lean is (a) bisect first — get the immediate symptom resolved, then make the platform choice with that data in hand. But this needs Misha's call.
details.blocking_input: Misha picks (a), (b), or (c) — and if (b)/(c), authorizes the scoping work.

items.text: Want me to scope the Vercel-migration comparison doc?

details.description: Before committing to (b) or (c), the orchestrator can produce a comparison doc (current host vs. Vercel: feature parity, cost model, ops surface, observability hooks, rollback story). The work is ~2-4 hours.
details.instructions: Reply: yes / no / not-yet. If yes, the orchestrator will draft the doc and surface it as a follow-up item with the comparison filled in.
details.recommendation: Worth doing IF Misha leans (b) or (c). Skip if (a) bisect-first.
details.blocking_input: Misha's go/no-go on scoping the doc

items.text: Subscribe to vercel-status.com so we get pinged on incidents

details.description: Independent of the migration decision, subscribing to Vercel status alerts is cheap insurance — if Vercel's hosted-routes platform is in our serving chain, we want a heads-up on incidents.
details.instructions: Go to vercel-status.com → Subscribe to Updates → enter Misha's email or a shared inbox. Choose channels (Email / RSS / Slack webhook).
details.recommendation: Email subscription to the on-call address; takes <60 seconds.
details.blocking_input: Misha completes subscription (no follow-up needed — small task, fire-and-forget).

annotations.text: RESOLVED (2026-05-28): root cause was NOT a Vercel/hosting problem � it was a Next.js dynamic-segment slug conflict ([id] vs [orgId]), fixed in code by PR #309. Migration is superseded + closed (ADR docs/decisions/063-vercel-stays-no-migration.md). Branch left open ONLY for the cause-independent action i-vercel-status-sub (subscribe to vercel-status.com).

items.text: S1 Auth & Access (P0): login/forgot-password repair, Google sign-in + post-invite refresh bug, "you already exist" re-invite block, bulk invite 1-of-4 � C-07/C-08/C-47/C-31
S2 Org Form Hardening (P0): Industry+Timezone dropdowns on /admin/orgs, hide slug field, jsdom+RTL component-test infra � WALKTHROUGH-001 = C-01/C-02
S3 Search/Save/Persistence (P0): fix contact search, contact-create persistence, Personal Data save no-op, member-role update, standardize save UX � C-12/C-41/C-21/C-32/C-35
S4 Contact Import Hardening (P0): address mapping, full-name split, per-row error reporting, phone + prefix � C-15/C-10/C-11/C-16 (overlaps S3 contact pages)
S5 CallRail Integration (P0): API scoping+wiring, RingCentral compat, demonstrable end-to-end for One Season � C-04/C-06 (biggest blocker)
Tier 2 (queued): Schedules & Availability, dynamic import auto-mapping (C-09), Comm-Prefs overhaul (C-25/26/27/28/29), AI-suggested message (C-17), test-orgs purge (C-03), misc UX bundle
Tier 3 (Architecture, Jaime): PRD draft from build doctrine + code audit, 22-contradiction guardrails ship, conversation engine principles
COORD � Cody: provide real contact export file (blocks S4 import verification)
COORD � Jaime: ship 22-contradiction guardrails + PRD draft (Tier 3) + CallRail/RingCentral docs C-05 (blocks S5)
COORD � Misha: review CallRail scope before merge (S5); decide which test orgs to purge (C-03)

DECIDED 2026-05-29: CallRail webhook auth = per-org unguessable URL token (matches Retell handler pattern; no HMAC because CallRail does not expose a usable signature mechanism). Documented in PR #371 scoping doc.
DECIDED 2026-05-29: RingCentral integration NOT needed � CallRail abstracts RingCentral. S5 scope reduced (C-06 dropped from S5 sub-items).

DECIDED 2026-05-29: Each email maps to ONE org + ONE user. C-47 implements as inline invite-UI warning � (a) "this email already has an account", (b) same-org vs different-org context with org name visible to inviter, (c) "move account here" affordance. No new screens.

Tier 2: Contact deletion/archive (verified gap � no DELETE handler at src/app/api/contacts/[id]/route.ts, bulk-action route supports only move_stage + send_message, no soft-delete fields, no UI delete button). Recommend soft-archive default. Owner-only with admin override per org policy. UI surfaces: contact detail (single), list (bulk), confirmation modal.

Tier 2: S8 � In-app support chatbot. Floating Help widget on every Circuit page. Phase 1 = Q&A over Support Pages MDX corpus. Phase 2 = tool-call extension (show-me-where-X-is, explain-this-error, summarize-org-state, whats-changed-recently). Uses Circuit LLM client. Per-user conversation history. Plugin interface for new tools. Was BLOCKED on S7 Phase 2 ? SHIPPED in PR #378 ? UNBLOCKED 2026-05-29.

DECIDED 2026-05-29: Contact deletion = soft-archive ONLY on contractor surface (archived_at, hidden from list/search, reversible by admin, audit trail preserved). Hard-delete reserved for verified CCPA requests via dedicated admin-only workflow (structured request log, 24-month retention, third-party vendor notification trigger). Hard-delete NOT exposed in regular contact UI.

HF-1 (Hotfix): disable test-org generation in prod. Circuit creating circuit-test* orgs daily. Session local_65df9ac4-433d-4465-9a1f-a24dfb42cdc0. 6 steps: identify source ? disable via PR ? count accumulated test orgs ? soft-archive them ? add guard against silent re-enable ? 24h regression watch.
OPEN (Misha decision): is the CCPA-request workflow in Tier 2 (pre-launch) or Tier 3? Circuit serves California contractors + California consumers; Misha leans Tier 2.
Pick a path for the WS demo — round-trip lock-in.
